title: Calyptridium umbellatum
taxonomy: Calyptridium umbellatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Montiaceae, which is a family of plants commonly known as the "miner's lettuce" family. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales, which is a larger group of flowering plants that also includes carnations and pinks. At the next level of classification, Calyptridium umbellatum is a member of the class Magnoliopsida, also known as the dicotyledons. The class is further grouped into the division Magnoliophyta, which is a broad category of flowering plants. Calyptridium umbellatum is also a member of the kingdom Plantae, which is the domain of all plant life.
uses: Calyptridium umbellatum, also known as the purple owl's clover, is a plant with potential medicinal uses. intro: Calyptridium umbellatum, also known as the sand verbena or starry eyed purple, is a low-growing, succulent perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Calyptridium umbellatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
